tagline: Analyze EMF readings for paranormal investigation
keywords: EMF, ghost hunting, paranormal investigation, electromagnetic
calcFn: var spike = reading - baseline; var significance; if (nearElectronics === 'Yes') significance = 'Likely electronic interference'; else if (spike > 5) significance = 'Significant unexplained spike'; else if (spike > 2) significance = 'Notable deviation - investigate'; else significance = 'Within normal range'; return {spike: fmt(spike,1), significance: significance};

faq.q: What EMF level indicates paranormal activity?
faq.a: Unexplained spikes of 2-7 mG with no electronic source nearby are considered noteworthy.
Wiring, appliances, cell phones, and power lines all create EMF fields.

formulas.name: EMF Spike
formulas.explanation: Deviation from normal background EMF levels.

tagline: Grade and score paranormal evidence quality
keywords: paranormal evidence, ghost evidence, investigation score
calcFn: var score = 0; score += emfSpike === 'Yes' ? 15 : 0; var evpScores = {'No': 0, 'Class C (barely audible)': 10, 'Class B (audible)': 20, 'Class A (clear)': 30}; score += evpScores[evp] || 0; var visScores = {'None': 0, 'Shadow/Mist': 10, 'Partial Apparition': 20, 'Full Apparition': 30}; score += visScores[visual] || 0; var tempScores = {'No': 0, 'Yes (5-10°F drop)': 10, 'Yes (10°F+ drop)': 15}; score += tempScores[temperature] || 0; score += Math.min(witnesses * 5, 10); var grade = score >= 70 ? 'A - Compelling' : score >= 50 ? 'B - Strong' : score >= 30 ? 'C - Moderate' : score >= 15 ? 'D - Weak' : 'F - Insufficient'; return {score: fmt(score,0), grade: grade};

faq.a: Multiple types of evidence (EMF, EVP, visual) corroborated by witnesses.
Yes, which is why multiple independent data points and proper controls matter.

formulas.name: Evidence Score
formulas.formula: Score = EMF + EVP + Visual + Temperature + Witness points
formulas.explanation: Composite score from multiple evidence types.

tagline: Assess paranormal activity probability for a location
keywords: haunted house, paranormal activity, ghost risk, haunted location
calcFn: var score = Math.min(age * 0.1, 15) + deaths * 10 + reports * 5 + (nearCemetery === 'Yes' ? 15 : 0); score = Math.min(score, 100); var category = score >= 60 ? 'High Activity' : score >= 35 ? 'Moderate Activity' : score >= 15 ? 'Low Activity' : 'Minimal'; return {riskScore: fmt(score,0), category: category};

faq.a: Allegedly: violent deaths, old buildings, proximity to burial sites, and repeated reports.

faq.q: Do old buildings have more activity?
faq.a: Older buildings have more reported sightings, though this may correlate with more history.

formulas.name: Risk Score
formulas.formula: Score = Age Factor + Deaths×10 + Reports×5 + Cemetery Bonus
formulas.explanation: Weighted sum of historical and environmental factors.

tagline: Analyze EVP recording frequencies for anomalies
keywords: EVP, electronic voice phenomenon, frequency, audio analysis
calcFn: var nyquist = parseFloat(sampleRate) * 1000 / 2; var range = peakFreq < 300 ? 'Low frequency (unusual for voice)' : peakFreq < 1000 ? 'Low voice range' : peakFreq < 3000 ? 'Normal voice range' : 'High frequency (unusual)'; var classification = duration > 2 ? 'Extended vocalization' : duration > 0.5 ? 'Word/phrase length' : 'Brief burst'; return {range: range, classification: classification, nyquist: fmt(nyquist,0)};

faq.q: What frequency range are EVPs typically found?
faq.a: Most alleged EVPs fall in the 200-4000 Hz range, similar to human speech.

faq.q: What sample rate should I record at?
faq.a: 44.1 kHz minimum. Higher rates capture more frequency information.

formulas.name: Nyquist Frequency
formulas.explanation: The highest frequency a digital recording can capture.

tagline: Correlate paranormal reports with lunar phases
keywords: full moon, lunar cycle, paranormal, moon phase, ghost activity
calcFn: var fullMoonDays = Math.round(daysInPeriod / 29.5) * 5; var expectedPct = (fullMoonDays / daysInPeriod) * 100; var actualPct = (fullMoonReports / totalReports) * 100; var ratio = actualPct / expectedPct; var correlation = ratio > 1.5 ? 'Strong lunar correlation' : ratio > 1.1 ? 'Slight correlation' : ratio < 0.7 ? 'Inverse correlation' : 'No significant correlation'; return {expectedPct: fmt(expectedPct,1), actualPct: fmt(actualPct,1), correlation: correlation};

faq.q: Does the full moon increase paranormal activity?
faq.a: Many investigators report more activity near full moons, though scientific evidence is limited.

faq.q: How long is the full moon period?
faq.a: The full moon lasts about 1 day, but many count ±2 days for the 'full moon window'.

formulas.name: Expected Random %
formulas.formula: Expected% = Full Moon Days / Total Days × 100
formulas.explanation: What percentage of reports you'd expect by random chance.

tagline: Calculate optimal spirit box sweep rate settings
keywords: spirit box, SB7, ghost box, sweep rate, ITC
calcFn: var fullSweep = (sweepSpeed * channelRange) / 1000; var sweepsPerMinute = 60 / fullSweep; var recommendation = sweepSpeed < 75 ? 'Very fast - may miss responses' : sweepSpeed <= 150 ? 'Good balance of speed and clarity' : 'Slow sweep - easier to analyze but fewer passes'; return {fullSweep: fmt(fullSweep,1), sweepsPerMinute: fmt(sweepsPerMinute,1), recommendation: recommendation};

faq.a: 75-150ms is common. Faster sweeps reduce radio bleed-through but may miss responses.

faq.q: AM or FM for spirit box?
faq.a: Both are used. FM has less static, AM may capture more anomalous audio.

formulas.name: Sweep Time
formulas.explanation: Total time to scan through all channels.

tagline: Interpret your SpO2 blood oxygen readings
keywords: SpO2, blood oxygen, pulse oximeter, oxygen saturation
calcFn: var altAdj = {'Sea Level': 0, '3000-5000 ft': -2, '5000-8000 ft': -3, '8000+ ft': -5}; var adj = altAdj[altitude] || 0; var effectiveSpo2 = spo2 - adj; var status = effectiveSpo2 >= 95 ? 'Normal' : effectiveSpo2 >= 90 ? 'Mildly Low' : effectiveSpo2 >= 85 ? 'Low - concerning' : 'Critical - seek help'; var hrStatus = heartRate < 60 ? 'Low (bradycardia)' : heartRate <= 100 ? 'Normal' : 'Elevated (tachycardia)'; var action = effectiveSpo2 < 90 ? 'Seek medical attention immediately' : effectiveSpo2 < 95 ? 'Monitor closely, consult doctor if persistent' : 'Normal, no action needed'; return {status: status, hrStatus: hrStatus, action: action};

faq.a: 95-100% at sea level. Below 90% is concerning and requires medical attention.
Yes, SpO2 naturally drops 2-5% at higher elevations.

formulas.name: Altitude Adjustment
formulas.explanation: SpO2 adjusted for altitude effects on normal oxygen levels.

tagline: Calculate your recommended daily fiber intake
keywords: fiber intake, dietary fiber, fiber needs, digestive health
calcFn: var rec; if (age <= 3) rec = 19; else if (age <= 8) rec = 25; else if (age <= 13) rec = sex === 'Male' ? 31 : 26; else if (age <= 50) rec = sex === 'Male' ? 38 : 25; else rec = sex === 'Male' ? 30 : 21; var minimum = calories * 0.014; var sources = fmt(rec/5,0) + ' apples or ' + fmt(rec/8,0) + ' cups of beans'; return {recommendation: fmt(rec,0), minimum: fmt(minimum,0), sources: sources};

faq.q: How much fiber do I need?
faq.a: 25g/day for women, 38g/day for men under 50. Most Americans only get 15g.

faq.q: What foods are high in fiber?
faq.a: Beans, lentils, whole grains, fruits, vegetables, and nuts.

formulas.name: Fiber per Calorie
formulas.explanation: At least 14 grams of fiber per 1,000 calories consumed.

tagline: Assess your joint mobility and flexibility health
keywords: joint mobility, flexibility, range of motion, joint health
calcFn: var shoulderScore = Math.min(shoulderROM * 4, 30); var hamScore = Math.min((hamstring + 10) * 1.5, 30); var hipScore = Math.min((hipFlexion - 45) * 0.44, 40); var ageBonus = age > 50 ? 5 : 0; var score = Math.min(shoulderScore + hamScore + hipScore + ageBonus, 100); var rating = score >= 80 ? 'Excellent' : score >= 60 ? 'Good' : score >= 40 ? 'Average' : 'Needs Improvement'; return {score: fmt(score,0), rating: rating};

faq.a: Good mobility reduces injury risk, improves posture, and maintains independence with age.
Daily stretching, yoga, foam rolling, and joint-specific mobility exercises.

formulas.name: Mobility Score
formulas.explanation: Composite score from three key mobility tests.

tagline: Assess your hearing range by age and exposure
keywords: hearing loss, frequency range, hearing test, decibel, audiogram
calcFn: var baseMax = 20000 - (age * 150); var exposureLoss = {'Low (quiet environment)': 0, 'Moderate (some loud events)': 500, 'High (concerts, machinery)': 1500, 'Very High (military, factory)': 3000}; var earbudLoss = earbuds > 4 ? 1000 : earbuds > 2 ? 500 : 0; var maxFreq = Math.max(baseMax - (exposureLoss[exposure]||0) - earbudLoss, 2000); var lossRisk = maxFreq < 8000 ? 'High' : maxFreq < 12000 ? 'Moderate' : 'Low'; var recommendation = maxFreq < 8000 ? 'Consider professional hearing evaluation' : maxFreq < 12000 ? 'Use hearing protection in loud environments' : 'Continue protecting your hearing'; return {maxFreq: fmt(maxFreq,0), lossRisk: lossRisk, recommendation: recommendation};

faq.q: What frequency range is normal hearing?
faq.a: 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz for young adults. High frequencies decline naturally with age.
At high volumes (over 85 dB), extended earbud use can accelerate hearing loss.

formulas.name: Max Frequency
formulas.explanation: Age-related decline plus noise exposure damage.

tagline: Score your allergy symptoms and severity
keywords: allergy score, allergy severity, hay fever, allergic reaction
calcFn: var sneezeScore = Math.min(sneezing, 10); var score = sneezeScore + congestion + eyeItch + interference; var severity = score >= 30 ? 'Severe' : score >= 20 ? 'Moderate-Severe' : score >= 12 ? 'Moderate' : score >= 5 ? 'Mild' : 'Minimal'; var action = score >= 20 ? 'See allergist, consider immunotherapy' : score >= 12 ? 'Daily antihistamine + nasal spray' : score >= 5 ? 'As-needed antihistamine' : 'Monitor symptoms'; return {score: fmt(score,0), severity: severity, action: action};

faq.q: When should I see an allergist?
faq.a: When OTC medications don't control symptoms or allergies impact daily life.
Allergy shots or sublingual tablets that desensitize you to allergens over time.

tagline: Convert between glasses and contact lens prescriptions
keywords: eye prescription, glasses, contacts, diopter, vertex distance
calcFn: var vd = vertexDistance / 1000; var contactSphere = sphere / (1 - vd * sphere); contactSphere = Math.round(contactSphere * 4) / 4; var contactCyl = cylinder; var note = Math.abs(sphere) > 4 ? 'Significant conversion difference - verify with eye doctor' : 'Minor conversion - verify with eye doctor'; return {contactSphere: fmt(contactSphere,2), contactCyl: fmt(contactCyl,2), note: note};

faq.q: Are glasses and contacts prescriptions different?
faq.a: Yes, contacts sit directly on the eye so the power differs, especially for higher prescriptions.

faq.q: Can I use my glasses prescription for contacts?
faq.a: No, you need a separate contact lens fitting and prescription from your eye doctor.

formulas.name: Vertex Conversion
formulas.formula: Contact Power = Glasses Power / (1 - d × Power)
formulas.explanation: Adjust for vertex distance between lens and eye.

tagline: Calculate how tariffs affect consumer prices
keywords: tariff impact, consumer price, import tax, tariff cost
calcFn: var tariffAmount = basePrice * (tariffRate/100); var priceIncrease = tariffAmount * (passThrough/100); var newPrice = basePrice + priceIncrease; return {tariffAmount: dollar(tariffAmount), priceIncrease: dollar(priceIncrease), newPrice: dollar(newPrice)};

faq.a: Yes, studies show 60-100% of tariff costs are passed to consumers.
The percentage of tariff cost that importers pass on to consumers vs absorbing.

formulas.name: Price Increase
formulas.explanation: Tariff amount adjusted by how much retailers pass to consumers.

tagline: Calculate anti-dumping duties on imported goods
keywords: anti-dumping, duty, import, trade remedy, dumping margin
calcFn: var dumpingMargin = Math.max(((fairValue - exportPrice) / exportPrice) * 100, 0); var dutyPerUnit = Math.max(fairValue - exportPrice, 0); var totalDuty = dutyPerUnit * quantity; return {dumpingMargin: fmt(dumpingMargin,1), dutyPerUnit: dollar(dutyPerUnit), totalDuty: dollar(totalDuty)};

faq.a: Selling goods in a foreign market below fair value or below home market price.
The duty equals the difference between fair value and the export price.

formulas.name: Dumping Margin
formulas.formula: Margin = (Fair Value - Export Price) / Export Price × 100
formulas.explanation: Percentage by which export price is below fair market value.

tagline: Calculate savings from using a Foreign Trade Zone
keywords: FTZ, free trade zone, trade zone savings, import duty
calcFn: var normalDuty = importValue * (dutyRate/100); var reexportSavings = normalDuty * (reexportPct/100); var deferralSavings = normalDuty * 0.05 * (warehouseMonths/12); var ftzDuty = normalDuty - reexportSavings - deferralSavings; var savings = normalDuty - ftzDuty; return {normalDuty: dollar(normalDuty), ftzDuty: dollar(ftzDuty), savings: dollar(savings)};

faq.q: What is a Foreign Trade Zone?
faq.a: A designated area where imported goods can be stored, assembled, or manufactured without paying duty until entering US commerce.
Companies importing large volumes, especially those re-exporting or needing inventory deferral.

formulas.name: FTZ Savings
formulas.formula: Savings = Re-export Duty Avoided + Duty Deferral Benefit
formulas.explanation: Eliminated duty on re-exports plus interest savings on deferred payments.

tagline: Calculate duties under tariff rate quota systems
keywords: TRQ, tariff rate quota, import quota, over-quota tariff
calcFn: var inQuotaQty = Math.min(quantity, quotaLimit); var overQuotaQty = Math.max(quantity - quotaLimit, 0); var inQuotaDuty = inQuotaQty * unitPrice * (inQuotaRate/100); var overQuotaDuty = overQuotaQty * unitPrice * (overQuotaRate/100); var totalDuty = inQuotaDuty + overQuotaDuty; var totalValue = quantity * unitPrice; var effectiveRate = (totalDuty / totalValue) * 100; return {inQuotaDuty: dollar(inQuotaDuty), overQuotaDuty: dollar(overQuotaDuty), totalDuty: dollar(totalDuty), effectiveRate: fmt(effectiveRate,1)};

faq.q: What is a tariff rate quota?
faq.a: A two-tier tariff: lower rate up to a quota limit, higher rate for quantities exceeding it.
Common for agricultural products like sugar, dairy, beef, and cotton.

formulas.name: Total Duty
formulas.explanation: Sum of duties at both rates based on quota allocation.

tagline: Determine if your product qualifies for preferential trade treatment
keywords: rules of origin, trade agreement, USMCA, origin determination
calcFn: var contentPct = (domesticValue / totalValue) * 100; var thresholds = {'USMCA (75% auto, 60% other)': 60, 'EU FTA (varies)': 55, 'CAFTA-DR (35%)': 35, 'General (50%)': 50}; var threshold = thresholds[agreement] || 50; var qualifies = contentPct >= threshold ? 'Yes - qualifies for preferential rate' : 'No - does not meet content threshold'; return {contentPct: fmt(contentPct,1), threshold: fmt(threshold,0), qualifies: qualifies};

faq.a: Criteria to determine where a product was made, affecting eligibility for lower tariff rates.

faq.q: Why do rules of origin matter?
faq.a: Products meeting origin rules can get preferential (lower or zero) tariff rates under trade agreements.

formulas.name: Regional Content
formulas.explanation: Percentage of product value from FTA member countries.

tagline: Estimate Section 301 tariff costs on Chinese imports
keywords: Section 301, China tariff, trade war, import duty, tariff cost
calcFn: var rates = {'List 1 (25%)': 25, 'List 2 (25%)': 25, 'List 3 (25%)': 25, 'List 4A (7.5%)': 7.5, 'List 4B (varies)': 15}; var tariffRate = rates[list] || 25; var perShipment = importValue * (tariffRate/100); var annualTariff = perShipment * quantity; return {tariffRate: fmt(tariffRate,1), perShipment: dollar(perShipment), annualTariff: dollar(annualTariff)};

faq.a: Additional tariffs imposed on Chinese goods due to unfair trade practices, ranging from 7.5-25%.
Some products qualify for exclusions. Check USTR.gov for current exclusion lists.

formulas.name: Annual Cost
formulas.formula: Cost = Import Value × Tariff Rate × Shipments
formulas.explanation: Per-shipment tariff multiplied by annual frequency.
